docs.title: Convergio AI
docs.text:
Unified Omnichannel CRM with AI-Powered Automation

Convergio AI is a modern, open-source platform that brings together email management, AI-powered automation, stream broadcasting, calendar scheduling, and workflow orchestration — all in one intelligent dashboard. Built with React, Express, and PostgreSQL, powered by Claude AI and n8n workflows.

  • Getting Started

    Install, configure, and run your first instance in minutes.

    Quickstart

  • Features

    AI email automation, StreamBoost, calendar, task management, and more.

    Explore features

  • API Reference

    Complete REST API reference with 80+ endpoints for emails, AI, tasks, and streams.

    API docs

  • Architecture

    System design, database schema, and technology stack overview.

    Platform overview

  • Integrations

    n8n workflows, Cal.com scheduling, and multi-platform posting.

    View integrations

  • Development

    Contributing guide, design system, and development setup.

    Start contributing

docs.title: Key capabilities
docs.text: Capability Description AI Email Automation Claude AI-powered auto-replies, email intelligence, and smart categorization StreamBoost YouTube live stream detection with cross-platform announcements to Discord, X, Instagram, Facebook AI Operations Copilot Natural language chat interface for email summaries, lead tracking, and task management Calendar & Scheduling Full calendar with Cal.com integration, meeting detection from emails Task Management Convert emails to tasks with priorities, assignments, and due dates Digital Audits AI-powered security and digital audit workflows n8n Workflows Automated IMAP → AI → SMTP → Database pipelines with visual workflow builder

docs.title: Tech stack
docs.text:
FrontendBackendAI & Automation
  • React 19 with TypeScript
  • Vite 6 build tooling
  • Tabler Design System with dark/light mode
  • Recharts for analytics visualization
  • TipTap rich text editor
  • Express 5 (Node.js 20+)
  • PostgreSQL with pg driver
  • JWT authentication with bcrypt
  • Swagger/OpenAPI documentation
  • Sharp for image processing
  • Anthropic Claude for AI responses
  • n8n for workflow automation
  • IMAPFlow for email synchronization
  • Cal.com for scheduling

A Digitech Nomads project. Produced by Instinct Bits Lab.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to Convergio AI are documented here.

The format follows Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Unreleased
docs.text: • Initial documentation site launch.

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
The Convergio AI API is a REST API built on Express 5. It accepts JSON request bodies and returns JSON responses. Full OpenAPI 3.0.3 documentation is available at /api-docs when the server is running.

Base URL:

http://localhost:3001/api

Production (via Netlify proxy):

https://convergioai.netlify.app/api

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
Most endpoints use JWT Bearer authentication. See Authentication for details.

Authorization: Bearer <jwt_token>

docs.title: Endpoint groups
docs.text: Section Endpoints Auth Description Authentication 5 Mixed Register, login, refresh, logout, profile Emails 4 No Email CRUD, AI analysis Tasks 4 No Task management AI 6 No Model selection, content generation, chat, intelligence Calendar 12 No Events, Cal.com sync, meeting detection Settings 25+ JWT Profile, security, billing, tokens StreamBoost 10+ No Stream state, captions, credentials, milestones Error Handling — — Status codes and error format

docs.title: Quick reference
docs.text:
# Health check
curl http://localhost:3001/api/health

# Login
curl -X POST http://localhost:3001/api/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email": "demo@digitechnomads.com", "password": "demo123"}'

# List emails
curl http://localhost:3001/api/emails?tag=Hello&limit=10

# Dashboard stats
curl http://localhost:3001/api/stats

docs.title: Get current model
docs.text:
GET /api/ai-model

Returns the currently active AI model.

docs.title: Set active model
docs.text:
POST /api/ai-model

{
  "model": "claude"
}

Available models: gemini, claude, qwen, qwen3

docs.title: Generate content
docs.text:
POST /api/ai/generate

Generate AI content with optional model override.

{
  "prompt": "Write a professional email response",
  "model": "claude"
}

docs.title: Chat (AI Copilot)
docs.text:
POST /api/ai/chat

Natural language chat endpoint. See AI Operations Copilot.

{
  "message": "Summarize my recent emails"
}

docs.title: Email intelligence
docs.text:
POST /api/ai/email-intelligence

AI analysis of email content for urgency, insights, and action items.

docs.title: Runtime API key management
docs.text:
Manage AI provider API keys at runtime without restarting the server:

Method Endpoint Description GET /api/api-keys List configured AI keys (status only) POST /api/api-keys Add or override an AI provider key DELETE /api/api-keys/{keyId} Remove a runtime key override

docs.title: n8n integration endpoints
docs.text:
Used by n8n workflows to interact with the AI system:

Method Endpoint Description POST /api/n8n/email-response Generate AI email response POST /api/n8n/digital-audit Generate AI audit report GET /api/n8n/model-info Get current model info

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: Convergio AI uses JWT (JSON Web Tokens) with database-backed sessions for authentication.

docs.title: Register
docs.text:
POST /api/auth/register

Create a new user account.

RequestResponse (201) 
{
  "email": "user@example.com",
  "password": "securepassword",
  "name": "John Doe"
}

{
  "token": "eyJhbGciOi...",
  "user": {
    "id": 1,
    "email": "user@example.com",
    "name": "John Doe"
  }
}

docs.title: Login
docs.text:
POST /api/auth/login

Authenticate with email and password. Returns a JWT and refresh token.

RequestResponse (200) 
{
  "email": "user@example.com",
  "password": "securepassword"
}

{
  "token": "eyJhbGciOi...",
  "refreshToken": "eyJhbGciOi...",
  "user": {
    "id": 1,
    "email": "user@example.com",
    "name": "John Doe"
  }
}

docs.title: Refresh token
docs.text:
POST /api/auth/refresh

Exchange a refresh token for a new access token.

docs.title: Logout
docs.text:
POST /api/auth/logout

Destroy the current session. Requires JWT.

docs.title: Get current user
docs.text:
GET /api/auth/me

Returns the authenticated user's profile. Requires JWT.

docs.title: Using the token
docs.text:
Include the JWT in the Authorization header:

Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOi...

docs.title: Security model
docs.text:
• JWT + database session double-check (valid JWT AND active session)
  • Session revocation is instant (delete session record)
  • Refresh tokens for seamless token renewal
  • Password hashing with bcrypt (cost factor 10)
  • Optional 2FA via TOTP with backup codes

docs.title: Events
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description GET /api/calendar/events List events (filterable by start, end, status, source) POST /api/calendar/events Create event (supports Cal.com booking upsert) GET /api/calendar/events/{id} Get event details PATCH /api/calendar/events/{id} Update event DELETE /api/calendar/events/{id} Delete event PATCH /api/calendar/events/by-calcom/{bookingId} Update by Cal.com booking ID GET /api/calendar/upcoming List upcoming events GET /api/calendar/stats Calendar statistics

docs.title: AI meeting detection
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description POST /api/calendar/detect Detect meeting signals in text POST /api/calendar/events/from-email Create event from detected meeting

docs.title: Cal.com
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description GET /api/calcom/settings Get Cal.com config status POST /api/calcom/settings Save Cal.com API key POST /api/calcom/sync Trigger booking sync GET /api/calcom/event-types List Cal.com event types

docs.title: List emails
docs.text:
GET /api/emails
Parameter Type Description tag string Filter by tag (Hello, Partners, Info, Support, Neo) status string Filter by status limit number Results per page (default: 50) offset number Pagination offset RequestResponse (200) 
curl "http://localhost:3001/api/emails?tag=Hello&limit=10"

[
  {
    "id": 1,
    "message_id": "<abc@mail.com>",
    "from_address": "lead@company.com",
    "from_name": "Jane Smith",
    "to_address": "hello@digitechnomads.com",
    "subject": "Partnership inquiry",
    "body_text": "...",
    "tag": "Hello",
    "direction": "inbound",
    "received_at": "2026-02-25T10:30:00Z"
  }
]

docs.title: Create email (webhook)
docs.text:
POST /api/emails

Used by n8n to push new emails. Auto-derives tag, creates task, and saves auto-reply.

docs.title: Get email with replies
docs.text:
GET /api/emails/{id}

Returns the email with associated auto-replies.

docs.title: AI email analysis
docs.text:
GET /api/emails/{id}/ai-analysis

Returns AI-generated analysis of the email content including urgency, action items, and insights.

docs.title: Auto-replies
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description GET /api/auto-replies List all auto-replies POST /api/auto-replies Save AI-generated reply

docs.title: Send email
docs.text:
POST /api/send-email

Send an email via SMTP from a selected inbox.

docs.title: IMAP sync
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description POST /api/sync Trigger IMAP sync across all inboxes GET /api/sync/status Get last sync timestamp

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text: The API uses standard HTTP status codes and returns JSON error responses.

docs.title: HTTP status codes
docs.text: Code Meaning Description 200 OK Request succeeded 201 Created Resource created 400 Bad Request Invalid parameters 401 Unauthorized Missing or invalid JWT 403 Forbidden Insufficient permissions 404 Not Found Resource not found 409 Conflict Duplicate (e.g., email already exists) 500 Internal Server Error Unexpected error

docs.title: Retry strategy
docs.text:
For 5xx errors, implement exponential backoff:

import time, requests

for attempt in range(3):
    response = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
    if response.status_code >= 500:
        time.sleep(2 ** attempt)
        continue
    break

docs.title: Settings API
docs.text: All settings endpoints require JWT authentication.

docs.title: Profile
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description GET /api/settings/profile Get user profile PATCH /api/settings/profile Update name, company, phone, timezone POST /api/settings/profile/avatar Update avatar URL PATCH /api/settings/profile/notifications Update notification preferences

docs.title: Security
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description POST /api/settings/security/password Change password GET /api/settings/security/2fa Get 2FA status POST /api/settings/security/2fa/setup Initialize 2FA (returns TOTP secret) POST /api/settings/security/2fa/verify Verify and enable 2FA POST /api/settings/security/2fa/disable Disable 2FA GET /api/settings/security/sessions List active sessions DELETE /api/settings/security/sessions/{id} Revoke a session GET /api/settings/security/api-keys List user API keys POST /api/settings/security/api-keys Create API key (cai_ prefix) DELETE /api/settings/security/api-keys/{id} Revoke API key GET /api/settings/security/login-history Login history (last 50)

docs.title: Billing
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description GET /api/settings/billing/subscription Current subscription GET /api/settings/billing/plans Available plans GET /api/settings/billing/payment-methods Payment methods GET /api/settings/billing/invoices Invoice history GET /api/settings/billing/usage Usage overview

docs.title: Tokens
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description GET /api/settings/tokens/balance Token balance GET /api/settings/tokens/usage Usage over time (7d/30d/90d) GET /api/settings/tokens/usage/by-model Usage by AI model GET /api/settings/tokens/packages Purchasable packages POST /api/settings/tokens/purchase Purchase tokens GET /api/settings/tokens/history Usage history (paginated) GET /api/settings/tokens/alerts List usage alerts POST /api/settings/tokens/alerts Create alert PATCH /api/settings/tokens/alerts/{id} Update alert DELETE /api/settings/tokens/alerts/{id} Delete alert

docs.title: Stream status
docs.text:
GET /api/streamboost/status

Returns the current stream state (live/ended, viewer count, peak viewers).

docs.title: Captions
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description POST /api/streamboost/captions/generate Generate AI captions for all platforms GET /api/streamboost/captions/pending List pending captions awaiting approval PATCH /api/streamboost/captions/pending Bulk update caption status POST /api/streamboost/captions/approve Approve or skip a caption POST /api/streamboost/captions/dispatch Post approved captions to platforms

docs.title: Platform credentials

docs.title: List tasks
docs.text:
GET /api/tasks
Parameter Type Description status string Filter: todo, in_progress, done priority string Filter: low, medium, high, urgent tag string Filter by email-derived tag

docs.title: Create task
docs.text:
POST /api/tasks

{
  "title": "Follow up with client",
  "description": "Regarding partnership proposal",
  "priority": "high",
  "email_id": 42
}

docs.title: Get task
docs.text: GET /api/tasks/{id}

docs.title: Update task
docs.text:
PATCH /api/tasks/{id}

{
  "status": "in_progress",
  "priority": "urgent"
}

GET /api/stats

Returns aggregate stats:

{

docs.title: Development
docs.text:
• Contributing

    Development workflow, coding standards, and PR guidelines.

    Contributing

  • Design System

    Color palette, typography, components, and theming.

    Design system

docs.title: Available scripts
docs.text:
npm run dev          # Frontend dev server (Vite, port 5173)
npm run server       # Backend API (Express, port 3001)
npm run dev:all      # Both concurrently
npm run build        # Production build
npm run preview      # Preview production build
npm run lint         # ESLint
npm run db:init      # Initialize database schemas
npm run db:migrate   # Run migrations
npm run db:seed      # Seed sample data

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: We welcome contributions from the community.

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository
  2. Create a feature branch: git checkout -b feature/your-feature
  3. Make changes following existing code patterns
  4. Test thoroughly: npm run lint
  5. Commit with conventional commit format
  6. Push and submit a Pull Request

docs.title: Code standards
docs.text:
• TypeScript for all new code (frontend and backend)
  • Follow existing patterns in the codebase
  • Meaningful commit messages
  • Update documentation for new features

docs.title: Areas for contribution
docs.text: Area Examples Frontend UI improvements, accessibility, responsive design Backend API enhancements, performance, new endpoints AI Better prompts, new model integrations Docs Guides, examples, API documentation Testing Unit tests, integration tests, E2E

docs.title: Project structure
docs.text:
• src/ — React frontend (components, pages, context, utils)
  • server/ — Express backend (routes, services, middleware)
  • schema*.sql — Database migrations
  • n8n-workflows/ — Automation workflow JSON files
  • knowledge-base/ — AI agent prompts and context

docs.title: Design System
docs.text: Convergio AI uses a Tabler-inspired design system with comprehensive dark/light mode support.

docs.title: Color palette
docs.text: Dark ModeLight Mode Token Value Usage Background #182433 Page background Cards #1d2838 Elevated surfaces Primary #f15b22 Orange accent Success #2fb344 Green indicators Warning #f59e0b Amber alerts Error #ef4444 Red errors Token Value Usage Background #ffffff Page background Cards #f8fafc Elevated surfaces Primary #ea580c Orange accent Success #16a34a Green indicators

docs.title: Typography
docs.text: Property Value Font family Inter (system fonts fallback) Scale 32px → 12px (6 levels) Line height 1.428 Weights 400, 500, 600, 700

docs.title: Component library
docs.text:
• Stat Cards — Metrics with sparklines and trend indicators
  • Icon Badges — Status indicators with color coding
  • Data Tables — Sortable, filterable tables
  • Modal Overlays — Detail views and forms
  • Activity Feeds — Timeline-style event lists
  • Tag System — Color-coded email categorization

docs.title: Customization
docs.text: All design tokens are defined as CSS custom properties in src/styles/variables.css. Override them for custom themes.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
Convergio AI provides a comprehensive suite of tools for managing business communications, content, and operations.

  • AI Email Automation

    Multi-inbox management with Claude AI-powered auto-replies and smart categorization.

    Learn more

  • AI Operations Copilot

    Natural language chat for email summaries, lead tracking, and task management.

    Learn more

  • StreamBoost

    YouTube live stream detection with AI captions and cross-platform posting.

    Learn more

  • Calendar & Scheduling

    Full calendar with Cal.com integration and AI meeting detection from emails.

    Learn more

  • Task Management

    Auto-created tasks from emails with priorities, filters, and status tracking.

    Learn more

  • Digital Audits

    AI-powered website and security audit workflows.

    Learn more

docs.title: AI Operations Copilot
docs.text: The AI Operations Copilot is a conversational interface embedded in the Convergio AI dashboard. It provides natural language access to your email data, tasks, and operational metrics.

docs.title: Capabilities
docs.text:
• Summarize emails — Get concise summaries of recent emails across all inboxes
  • Identify urgent items — Surface emails and tasks that need immediate attention
  • Track leads — Overview of leads and business opportunities from the Hello inbox
  • Task summaries — Status of all tasks with priority breakdown
  • General questions — Ask anything about your data

docs.title: Quick actions
docs.text:
The copilot includes pre-built quick actions:

Action Prompt Summarize emails "Summarize my recent emails" Urgent items "What urgent items need my attention?" Leads overview "Show me leads and opportunities" Task summary "Give me a task summary"

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
The copilot uses the /api/ai/chat endpoint, which:

  1. Accepts a natural language message
  2. Queries relevant data from the database (emails, tasks, stats)
  3. Passes context to the active AI model (Claude, Gemini, or Qwen)
  4. Returns a structured response

docs.title: Multi-model support
docs.text:
The copilot respects the globally selected AI model. Switch models at any time via the AI model selector:

  • Google Gemini — Fast responses, good for general queries
  • Anthropic Claude — High quality reasoning, best for complex analysis
  • Qwen 480B — Alternative via OpenRouter
  • Qwen3-8B — Lightweight option via Bytez

docs.title: Calendar & Scheduling
docs.text: Convergio AI includes a full-featured calendar with multiple view modes, Cal.com integration for booking management, and AI-powered meeting detection from email content.

docs.title: Calendar views
docs.text: View Description Month Traditional month grid with event dots Week 7-day view with time slots Day Detailed single-day schedule List Chronological event list

docs.title: Event sources
docs.text:
Events can come from three sources:

Source How it works Manual Created directly in the calendar UI Cal.com Synced from Cal.com bookings via API Email detected AI detects meeting references in emails and suggests events

docs.title: Cal.com integration
docs.text:
Connect your Cal.com account to automatically sync bookings:

  1. Go to Settings → enter your Cal.com API key
  2. Enable sync — bookings are pulled periodically
  3. New bookings appear as calendar events with source: calcom
  4. Event types from Cal.com are available in the dashboard

docs.title: Sync process
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant CalCom as Cal.com API
    participant n8n as n8n Sync
    participant API as Express API
    participant DB as PostgreSQL

    n8n->>CalCom: Fetch new bookings
    CalCom-->>n8n: Booking data
    n8n->>API: POST /api/calendar/events
    API->>DB: Upsert by calcom_booking_id

docs.title: AI meeting detection
docs.text:
The meeting detector service analyzes email text using NLP-based keyword scoring:

  • Detects date/time references
  • Identifies meeting-related language
  • Assigns a confidence score
  • Suggests creating a calendar event

Use the /api/calendar/detect endpoint to analyze any text for meeting signals, or /api/calendar/events/from-email to create an event directly from a detected meeting.

docs.title: Digital Audits
docs.text: Convergio AI includes an AI-powered audit system for analyzing websites and generating comprehensive digital presence reports.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
1. Enter a website URL and optional business details
  2. The audit request is sent to the n8n digital audit workflow
  3. n8n orchestrates the analysis (security checks, SEO, performance)
  4. AI generates a structured audit report with an overall score
  5. Results are saved and displayed in the dashboard

docs.title: Audit types
docs.text: Type Description Digital Audit Comprehensive website analysis with AI-generated recommendations Security Audit Focused security assessment

docs.title: Audit results
docs.text:
Results include:

  • Overall score — Aggregate assessment (0-100)
  • Detailed results — Stored as JSONB for flexible reporting
  • Business context — Original business details provided with the request
  • Status tracking — pending → processing → completed

docs.title: API
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description GET /api/audits List all audits POST /api/audits Create new audit (triggers n8n) GET /api/audits/{id} Get audit details POST /api/audits/{id}/complete Complete audit with results (n8n callback) GET /api/security-audits List security audits POST /api/security-audits Run security audit GET /api/security-audits/{id} Get security audit details

docs.title: AI Email Automation (CommBoost)
docs.text: CommBoost is Convergio AI's intelligent email management system. It connects to multiple IMAP inboxes, automatically categorizes incoming emails, generates AI-powered responses, and syncs everything through n8n workflows.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant IMAP as Email Server
    participant n8n as n8n Workflow
    participant AI as Claude / Gemini
    participant API as Express API
    participant DB as PostgreSQL
    participant UI as Dashboard

    n8n->>IMAP: Fetch new emails (IMAP)
    IMAP-->>n8n: New emails
    n8n->>AI: Generate auto-reply
    AI-->>n8n: Reply content
    n8n->>API: POST /api/emails (webhook)
    API->>DB: Save email + auto-reply + create task
    n8n->>IMAP: Send reply (SMTP)
    UI->>API: GET /api/emails
    API-->>UI: Email list with replies

docs.title: Multi-inbox system
docs.text:
Each inbox maps to a tag and its own AI knowledge base:

Inbox Tag Purpose AI personality hello@ Hello Sales and new business leads Professional, consultative partners@ Partners Partnership inquiries Collaborative, strategic info@ Info Press and general information Informative, media-friendly support@ Support Client support tickets Helpful, empathetic neo@ Neo Technical inquiries Technical, precise

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Auto-categorization — Emails are tagged automatically based on the recipient address
  • AI auto-replies — Claude or Gemini generates contextual responses using inbox-specific knowledge bases
  • Email intelligence — AI analysis endpoint provides insights on email content, urgency, and action items
  • Compose and reply — Rich text editor (TipTap) for composing and replying directly from the dashboard
  • IMAP sync — On-demand synchronization across all configured inboxes
  • Deduplication — Unique message_id constraint prevents duplicate email storage
  • Attachment handling — Attachment metadata stored as JSONB

docs.title: Dashboard views
docs.text:
• Unified inbox — All emails across all inboxes in one view
  • Per-inbox view — Filter by specific inbox (/dashboard/inbox/hello, etc.)
  • Tag filtering — Filter by auto-derived tags
  • Status filtering — Filter by read/unread, replied/pending status

docs.title: StreamBoost
docs.text: StreamBoost automates the entire live stream announcement pipeline — from detecting a YouTube live stream to posting AI-generated, platform-specific announcements across Discord, X (Twitter), Instagram, and Facebook.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant YT as YouTube API
    participant n8n as n8n Detector
    participant API as Express API
    participant AI as Claude AI
    participant UI as Dashboard
    participant Platforms as Discord / X / Meta

    n8n->>YT: Poll for live streams (every 3 min)
    YT-->>n8n: Live stream detected!
    n8n->>API: Update stream state
    API->>AI: Generate platform captions
    AI-->>API: Captions (per platform)
    API-->>UI: Show pending captions
    UI->>API: User approves / edits
    API->>Platforms: Dispatch announcements

docs.title: Stream lifecycle
docs.text: Phase Event Actions Detection YouTube live detected Create stream state, generate go-live captions Live Stream ongoing Monitor viewer count, track peak viewers Milestone Subscriber threshold hit Generate celebration captions End Stream ends Generate end-stream CTA captions

docs.title: Platform support
docs.text: Platform Method Format Discord Webhook Rich embed or plain text X (Twitter) OAuth2 API Tweet with hashtags Instagram Meta Graph API Post with image Facebook Meta Graph API Page post

docs.title: Caption approval flow
docs.text:
1. AI generates captions tailored to each platform's voice and format
  2. Captions appear in the StreamBoost dashboard as pending
  3. You can:
    • Approve — Post as-is
    • Edit — Modify the text before posting
    • Skip — Don't post to that platform
  4. Approved captions are dispatched via the n8n posting pipeline

docs.title: Channel voice settings
docs.text:
Each platform has configurable AI tone settings:

Setting Options Description Tone preset professional, friendly, hype, mixed Base AI personality Custom prompt Free text Full prompt override Core hashtags Array Always-included hashtags CTA text Free text Call-to-action appended to posts Discord format embed / plain Discord-specific formatting

docs.title: Milestone celebrations
docs.text:
StreamBoost tracks subscriber milestones and automatically generates celebration announcements:

Milestone Triggered at 100 Subscribers 100 500 Subscribers 500 1K Subscribers 1,000 5K Subscribers 5,000 10K Subscribers 10,000 50K Subscribers 50,000

Custom thresholds can be added via the API.

docs.title: Task Management (WorkBoost)
docs.text: WorkBoost automatically converts every incoming email into an actionable task, ensuring nothing falls through the cracks.

docs.title: Auto-task creation
docs.text:
When an email is saved to the database (via the n8n webhook or IMAP sync), a task is automatically created:

  • Title — Derived from the email subject
  • Description — Links back to the source email
  • Status — Set to todo by default
  • Priority — Default medium, adjustable

docs.title: Task lifecycle
docs.text: todo → in_progress → done

docs.title: Filtering and views
docs.text: Filter Options Status todo, in_progress, done Priority low, medium, high, urgent Tag Email-derived tag (Hello, Partners, etc.)

docs.title: API
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description GET /api/tasks List tasks with filters POST /api/tasks Create a new task GET /api/tasks/{id} Get task details PATCH /api/tasks/{id} Update status or priority

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
Everything you need to go from zero to a running Convergio AI instance.

  • Quickstart

    Clone, configure, and launch in under 10 minutes.

    Quickstart

  • Installation

    Detailed setup for Node.js, PostgreSQL, and n8n.

    Install

  • Configuration

    Environment variables, API keys, and database setup.

    Configure

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Requirement Minimum version Node.js 20.0+ PostgreSQL 15+ npm 9.0+ n8n 1.0+ (optional)

You'll also need:

  • An Anthropic Claude API key for AI features
  • An email account with IMAP/SMTP access
  • A Cal.com account (optional, for scheduling)

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: All configuration is managed through environment variables. Copy .env.example to .env and fill in the values.

docs.title: Database
docs.text: Variable Description Example DATABASE_URL PostgreSQL connection string postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/convergioai

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: Variable Description Example JWT_SECRET Secret key for JWT signing A random 64-char string

docs.title: AI
docs.text: Variable Description Example ANTHROPIC_API_KEY Anthropic Claude API key sk-ant-api03-...

docs.title: Email (IMAP/SMTP)
docs.text: Variable Description Example IMAP_HOST IMAP server hostname imap.gmail.com IMAP_PORT IMAP server port 993 SMTP_HOST SMTP server hostname smtp.gmail.com SMTP_PORT SMTP server port 587 EMAIL_USER Email account you@example.com EMAIL_PASS Email password/app password your-app-password

docs.title: n8n Integration
docs.text: Variable Description Example N8N_BASE_URL n8n instance base URL http://localhost:5678

docs.title: Cal.com Integration
docs.text: Variable Description Example CALCOM_API_KEY Cal.com API key cal_live_...

docs.title: Server
docs.text: Variable Description Default PORT Backend API port 3001 NODE_ENV Runtime environment development

docs.title: Subscription plans
docs.text:
The database is seeded with three default plans:

Plan Monthly (INR) Token limit Features Free 0 1,000 100 emails/month, 50 AI responses Pro 1,999 50,000 5,000 emails, 2,500 AI responses Enterprise 9,999 500,000 Unlimited emails/AI, dedicated support

docs.title: Token packages
docs.text:
Package Tokens Price (INR) Bonus tokens Starter 5,000 499 0 Growth 25,000 1,999 2,500 Business 100,000 6,999 15,000 Enterprise 500,000 29,999 100,000

Security

Never commit .env files to version control. The .gitignore already excludes them.

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
• Architecture — Understand the system design
  • API Reference — Explore all endpoints

docs.title: System requirements
docs.text: Component Requirement Node.js 20.0 or higher PostgreSQL 15 or higher npm 9.0 or higher OS macOS, Linux, Windows

docs.title: Install Node.js dependencies
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/InstinctBits/convergioai.git
cd convergioai
npm install

docs.title: Set up PostgreSQL
docs.text:
Create the database:

createdb convergioai

Initialize all schemas:

npm run db:init

This executes four schema files in order:

  1. schema.sql — Core tables (emails, tasks, auto-replies, app settings)
  2. schema-settings.sql — Users, auth, billing, API keys, token management
  3. schema-calendar.sql — Calendar events, Cal.com settings
  4. schema-streamboost.sql — Stream state, announcements, platform credentials, captions

docs.title: Start the servers
docs.text:
# Start both frontend and backend concurrently
npm run dev:all

# Or start them separately
npm run dev      # Frontend (Vite) — port 5173
npm run server   # Backend (Express) — port 3001

docs.title: Method 2: Docker
docs.text:
# Build and start all services
docker-compose up -d

# Access the application
# Frontend: http://localhost:5173
# Backend:  http://localhost:3001

docs.title: Method 3: Production with PM2
docs.text:
The repository includes a PM2 ecosystem config:

# Install PM2 globally
npm install -g pm2

# Build the frontend
npm run build

# Start with PM2
pm2 start pm2.ecosystem.config.cjs

docs.title: n8n workflow setup (optional)
docs.text:
n8n powers the automated email processing pipeline. To set it up:

  1. Install n8n (self-hosted or cloud)
  2. Import the workflow files from n8n-workflows/:
  3. email-agent-workflow.json — IMAP → AI → SMTP pipeline
  4. calcom-calendar-sync.json — Cal.com booking sync
  5. digital-audit-workflow.json — Security audit automation
  6. 01_YouTube_Live_Detector.json — StreamBoost live detection
  7. 02_Cross_Posting_AI.json — Multi-platform announcements
  8. Configure credentials in n8n for your email, Claude API, and database
  9. Activate the workflows

docs.title: Verify installation
docs.text:
# Check the API health
curl http://localhost:3001/api/health

Expected response:

{
  "status": "ok",
  "version": "3.0.0"
}

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
• Configuration — Environment variables and API key setup
  • Features — Explore platform capabilities

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text: Get a fully functional Convergio AI instance running on your machine.

docs.title: 1. Clone the repository
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/InstinctBits/convergioai.git
cd convergioai

docs.title: 2. Install dependencies
docs.text: npm install

docs.title: 3. Configure environment
docs.text:
cp .env.example .env

Edit .env with your credentials:

# Database
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:password@localhost:5432/convergioai

# AI
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...

# Email (IMAP/SMTP)
IMAP_HOST=imap.gmail.com
IMAP_PORT=993
SMTP_HOST=smtp.gmail.com
SMTP_PORT=587
EMAIL_USER=your@email.com
EMAIL_PASS=your-app-password

# JWT
JWT_SECRET=your-secret-key

docs.title: 4. Initialize the database
docs.text:
npm run db:init

This runs the SQL schemas (schema.sql, schema-settings.sql, schema-calendar.sql, schema-streamboost.sql) to create all required tables.

docs.title: 5. Start the development servers
docs.text:
npm run dev:all

This starts both servers concurrently:

Service URL Description Frontend http://localhost:5173 React dashboard Backend http://localhost:3001 Express API server

docs.title: 6. Access the dashboard
docs.text:
Open http://localhost:5173 in your browser. You'll see the login page.

A demo account is seeded automatically:

Field Value Email demo@digitechnomads.com Password demo123

docs.title: What's next?
docs.text:
• Installation — Detailed setup including Docker and n8n
  • Configuration — All environment variables and options
  • Features — Explore what Convergio AI can do

docs.title: Guides
docs.text:
Practical, step-by-step guides for common workflows.

  • Deployment

    Deploy Convergio AI in production environments.

    Deployment guide

  • Best Practices

    Production patterns for reliability, security, and performance.

    Best practices

docs.title: Recommended stack
docs.text: Component Platform Description Frontend Netlify / Vercel Static build with API proxy Backend VPS with PM2 Express API server Database PostgreSQL Remote or managed Workflows Self-hosted n8n Automation engine

docs.title: Frontend (Netlify)
docs.text:
The netlify.toml is pre-configured:

npm run build
# Deploy dist/ to Netlify

Key settings: - Build command: npm run build - Publish directory: dist - API proxy: /api/* redirects to your VPS

docs.title: Backend (PM2 on VPS)
docs.text:
# Install PM2
npm install -g pm2

# Start the API server
pm2 start pm2.ecosystem.config.cjs

# Save and enable startup
pm2 save
pm2 startup

docs.title: Docker
docs.text: docker-compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml up -d

docs.title: Environment
docs.text: Set all configuration variables in your production environment. Use a secrets manager for API keys.

docs.title: Self-Hosting
docs.text: Convergio AI is fully open-source and designed to run on your own infrastructure.

docs.title: Minimum requirements
docs.text: Resource Recommendation CPU 2 vCPU RAM 2 GB Storage 20 GB SSD OS Ubuntu 22.04+

docs.title: Setup checklist
docs.text:
1. Install Node.js 20+, PostgreSQL 15+
  2. Clone the repository
  3. Configure .env with your credentials
  4. Run npm run db:init to create tables
  5. Build frontend: npm run build
  6. Start with PM2: pm2 start pm2.ecosystem.config.cjs
  7. Set up reverse proxy (nginx) with SSL
  8. Install and configure n8n for workflow automation
  9. Configure DNS and firewall rules

docs.title: n8n self-hosting
docs.text:
n8n can run alongside the API on the same VPS:

npm install -g n8n
n8n start

Or use Docker: docker run -d --name n8n n8nio/n8n

Import the workflow JSON files from n8n-workflows/ and configure credentials.

docs.title: Integrations
docs.text:
Convergio AI connects to external services through n8n workflows and direct API integrations.

  • n8n Workflows

    Automated pipelines for email processing, stream detection, and audits.

    n8n setup

  • Cal.com

    Booking sync and scheduling integration.

    Cal.com setup

  • Platform Connections

    YouTube, Discord, X, Instagram, and Facebook integrations.

    Platforms

docs.title: Cal.com Integration
docs.text: Sync your Cal.com bookings directly into the Convergio AI calendar.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Get your Cal.com API key from cal.com/settings/developer
  2. In Convergio AI, go to Settings or use the API:
curl -X POST http://localhost:3001/api/calcom/settings \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"apiKey": "cal_live_..."}'
  1. Trigger a sync: POST /api/calcom/sync
  2. Bookings appear as calendar events with source: calcom

docs.title: API endpoints
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description GET /api/calcom/settings Check connection status POST /api/calcom/settings Save API key POST /api/calcom/sync Trigger booking sync GET /api/calcom/event-types List your Cal.com event types

docs.title: Automated sync
docs.text: Use the calcom-calendar-sync.json n8n workflow for periodic automatic syncing.

docs.title: n8n Workflows
docs.text: Convergio AI uses n8n as its workflow automation engine. Pre-built workflow JSON files are included in the n8n-workflows/ directory.

docs.title: Available workflows
docs.text: Workflow File Description Email Agent email-agent-workflow.json IMAP fetch → AI analysis → Auto-reply → SMTP send → API webhook YouTube Live Detector 01_YouTube_Live_Detector.json Polls YouTube Data API every 3 minutes for live streams Cross-Platform Poster 02_Cross_Posting_AI.json Posts approved captions to Discord, X, Instagram, Facebook Shorts Generator 03_Shorts_Generator.json Content repurposing workflow Discord XP System 04_Discord_XP_System.json Community engagement tracking Calendar Sync calcom-calendar-sync.json Syncs Cal.com bookings to calendar events Digital Audit digital-audit-workflow.json AI-powered website audit pipeline

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Install n8n (self-hosted or n8n Cloud)
  2. Import workflow JSON files via n8n UI (Settings → Import)
  3. Configure credentials:
    • IMAP/SMTP for email
    • Anthropic Claude API key
    • HTTP endpoint pointing to your Express API
    • YouTube Data API key (for StreamBoost)
  4. Activate workflows

docs.title: Webhook endpoints
docs.text:
n8n communicates with the API through these webhook endpoints:

Endpoint Used by Direction POST /api/emails Email Agent n8n → API POST /api/auto-replies Email Agent n8n → API POST /api/n8n/email-response Email Agent API → n8n (AI) POST /api/n8n/digital-audit Digital Audit API → n8n (AI) POST /api/audits/{id}/complete Digital Audit n8n → API GET /api/n8n/model-info All n8n → API

docs.title: Platform Connections
docs.text: StreamBoost posts announcements to multiple platforms. Each requires its own credentials.

docs.title: Supported platforms
docs.text: Platform Credential type Used for YouTube API key Live stream detection via Data API v3 Discord Webhook URL Rich embed or plain text announcements X (Twitter) OAuth2 Tweet posting Meta (Instagram/Facebook) System user token Page/profile posting via Graph API

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Manage credentials via the StreamBoost settings page or the API:

# Check connection status
curl http://localhost:3001/api/streamboost/credentials

# Add credentials
curl -X POST http://localhost:3001/api/streamboost/credentials \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"platform": "discord", "credential_value": "https://discord.com/api/webhooks/..."}'

docs.title: Per-platform voice settings
docs.text:
Customize the AI tone for each platform:

Platform Default tone Format Discord Friendly Embed X Mixed Tweet with hashtags Instagram Hype Post with image Facebook Professional Page post

docs.title: Platform
docs.text:
Dive into the technical foundation of Convergio AI.

  • Architecture

    System design, service topology, and data flow.

    Architecture

  • Core Concepts

    Key abstractions — inboxes, tags, tokens, boosts, and workflows.

    Concepts

  • Database Schema

    PostgreSQL tables, relationships, and indexing strategy.

    Database

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: Convergio AI is a full-stack TypeScript application with a React frontend, Express backend, PostgreSQL database, and n8n workflow automation.

docs.title: High-level overview
docs.text:
graph TB
    subgraph Frontend ["Frontend (React + Vite)"]
        Dashboard[Dashboard]
        Boost[Boost Suite]
        Calendar[Calendar]
        Settings[Settings]
        AICopilot[AI Copilot]
    end

    subgraph Backend ["Backend (Express 5)"]
        Auth[Auth & JWT]
        EmailAPI[Email API]
        TaskAPI[Task API]
        AIAPI[AI API]
        CalAPI[Calendar API]
        StreamAPI[StreamBoost API]
        SettingsAPI[Settings API]
    end

    subgraph Automation ["n8n Workflows"]
        EmailAgent[Email Agent]
        LiveDetector[YouTube Live Detector]
        CrossPost[Cross-Platform Poster]
        CalSync[Cal.com Sync]
        AuditWF[Digital Audit]
    end

    subgraph External ["External Services"]
        Claude[Anthropic Claude]
        Gemini[Google Gemini]
        YouTube[YouTube Data API]
        Discord[Discord Webhooks]
        Meta[Meta Graph API]
        XAPI[X / Twitter API]
        CalCom[Cal.com API]
    end

    subgraph Data ["Data Layer"]
        PG[(PostgreSQL)]
    end

    Frontend <-->|REST API| Backend
    Backend <--> PG
    Automation -->|Webhooks| Backend
    Backend --> Claude
    Backend --> Gemini
    Automation --> YouTube
    Automation --> Discord
    Automation --> Meta
    Automation --> XAPI
    Automation --> CalCom

docs.title: Technology stack
docs.text: FrontendBackendAI & Automation Technology Version Purpose React 19.2 UI framework TypeScript 5.9 Type safety Vite 7.2 Build tooling and dev server React Router 7.13 Client-side routing Recharts 3.7 Data visualization TipTap 3.20 Rich text editor (email compose) Tabler — Design system foundation Technology Version Purpose Node.js 20+ Runtime Express 5.2 HTTP framework TypeScript 5.9 Type safety (via tsx) PostgreSQL 15+ Primary database pg 8.17 Database driver JWT — Authentication bcrypt 6.0 Password hashing Swagger UI 5.0 API documentation Sharp 0.34 Image processing IMAPFlow 1.2 Email synchronization Technology Purpose Anthropic Claude Primary AI for email replies and content Google Gemini Alternative AI model Qwen (OpenRouter) Additional model option n8n Workflow automation engine Cal.com Scheduling integration

docs.title: Multi-model AI adapter
docs.text:
The callAI() function routes requests to the active AI provider based on runtime configuration:

graph LR
    Request --> Router[callAI Router]
    Router -->|gemini| Gemini[Google Gemini]
    Router -->|claude| Claude[Anthropic Claude]
    Router -->|qwen| OpenRouter[Qwen via OpenRouter]
    Router -->|qwen3| Bytez[Qwen3 via Bytez]

Users can switch the active model at runtime via /api/ai-model without restarting the server.

docs.title: Email tag derivation
docs.text:
Emails are automatically categorized by the recipient address prefix:

Inbox address Derived tag Knowledge base hello@digitechnomads.com Hello Sales / new business partners@digitechnomads.com Partners Partnerships info@digitechnomads.com Info Press / general support@digitechnomads.com Support Client support neo@digitechnomads.com Neo Technical inquiries

docs.title: Auto-task creation
docs.text: Every incoming email automatically creates a corresponding task via saveEmailToDB(), ensuring nothing falls through the cracks.

docs.title: JWT + database sessions
docs.text:
Authentication uses a double-check pattern:

  1. JWT token must be valid (signature + expiry)
  2. Session record must exist and be active in the database

This allows instant session revocation without waiting for JWT expiry.

docs.title: Deployment architecture
docs.text:
graph LR
    User[User Browser] -->|HTTPS| Netlify[Netlify CDN]
    Netlify -->|/api/* proxy| VPS[VPS - Express API]
    VPS --> PG[(PostgreSQL)]
    n8n[n8n Instance] -->|Webhooks| VPS
    n8n --> YouTube[YouTube API]
    n8n --> Discord[Discord]
    n8n --> Email[IMAP/SMTP]
Component Platform Frontend Netlify Backend VPS with PM2 Database PostgreSQL (remote) Workflows Self-hosted n8n

docs.title: Boost Suite
docs.text:
Convergio AI organizes its features into a "Boost Suite" — a collection of focused tools that each enhance a specific aspect of business operations:

Boost Page Description CommBoost /dashboard/commboost Multi-inbox email management with AI auto-replies WorkBoost /dashboard/workboost Task management with priorities and assignments StreamBoost /dashboard/streamboost YouTube live stream automation and cross-platform posting ContentBoost /dashboard/contentboost AI-powered content creation tools IdeaBoost /dashboard/ideaboost Brainstorming and ideation CampaignBoost /dashboard/campaignboost Campaign management

docs.title: Inboxes and tags
docs.text:
Convergio AI supports multiple email inboxes, each mapped to a tag derived from the recipient address. Tags determine:

  • Which section of the dashboard displays the email
  • Which AI system prompt and knowledge base is used for auto-replies
  • How tasks are categorized

docs.title: AI tokens
docs.text:
AI usage is tracked through a token system:

  • Token balance — Current available tokens for the user
  • Token usage — Per-request tracking with model attribution
  • Token packages — Purchasable bundles (Starter, Growth, Business, Enterprise)
  • Usage alerts — Configurable threshold notifications

docs.title: Captions and approval flow
docs.text:
In StreamBoost, when a live stream is detected:

  1. AI generates platform-specific captions (one per target platform)
  2. Captions enter a pending state in the dashboard
  3. User can approve, edit, or skip each caption
  4. Approved captions are dispatched to the target platform (Discord, X, Instagram, Facebook)

docs.title: Workflows
docs.text:
n8n workflows automate background processes:

  • Email Agent — IMAP fetch → AI analysis → Auto-reply generation → SMTP send → Database storage
  • YouTube Live Detector — Polls YouTube API → Detects live streams → Triggers caption generation
  • Cross-Platform Poster — Takes approved captions → Posts to Discord/X/Meta
  • Calendar Sync — Syncs Cal.com bookings → Creates calendar events
  • Digital Audit — Runs website analysis → Generates audit report

docs.title: Sessions and security
docs.text:
• Sessions are stored in the database with device info, IP, and expiry
  • 2FA uses TOTP with backup codes
  • API keys use a cai_ prefix with hashed storage
  • Login history tracks all authentication attempts

docs.title: Database Schema
docs.text: Convergio AI uses PostgreSQL with four schema files, applied in order during npm run db:init.

docs.title: Schema files
docs.text: File Tables Domain schema.sql 4 Core: emails, tasks, auto-replies, audits schema-settings.sql 14 Auth, billing, tokens schema-calendar.sql 2 Calendar events, Cal.com schema-streamboost.sql 7 Streams, announcements, captions

docs.title: emails
docs.text: Column Type Description id SERIAL PK message_id VARCHAR(500) UNIQUE Email message ID (deduplication) from_address VARCHAR(255) Sender email from_name VARCHAR(255) Sender display name to_address VARCHAR(255) Recipient email subject TEXT Email subject line body_text TEXT Plain text body body_html TEXT HTML body tag VARCHAR(50) Auto-derived category direction VARCHAR(20) inbound or outbound attachments JSONB Attachment metadata received_at TIMESTAMPTZ When the email was received

docs.title: tasks
docs.text: Column Type Description id SERIAL PK email_id INTEGER FK Link to source email title VARCHAR(500) Task title description TEXT Task details status VARCHAR(50) todo, in_progress, done priority VARCHAR(20) low, medium, high, urgent completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ Completion timestamp

docs.title: digital_audits
docs.text: Column Type Description id SERIAL PK website_url VARCHAR(500) Target website status VARCHAR(50) Audit status overall_score INTEGER Aggregate score audit_results JSONB Detailed results business_details JSONB Business context

docs.title: users
docs.text: Core user table with email, password hash, profile fields, timezone, and notification preferences.

docs.title: Related tables
docs.text:
erDiagram
    users ||--o{ user_sessions : has
    users ||--o| user_2fa : has
    users ||--o{ api_keys : owns
    users ||--o{ login_history : generates
    users ||--o| user_subscriptions : subscribes
    users ||--o{ payment_methods : adds
    users ||--o{ invoices : receives
    users ||--o| token_balances : has
    users ||--o{ token_usage : tracks
    users ||--o{ token_purchases : makes
    users ||--o{ usage_alerts : configures
    subscription_plans ||--o{ user_subscriptions : offers
    token_packages ||--o{ token_purchases : sells

docs.title: Subscription plans (seeded)
docs.text: Plan Slug Monthly (INR) Token limit Free free 0 1,000 Pro pro 1,999 50,000 Enterprise enterprise 9,999 500,000

docs.title: calendar_events
docs.text:
Full-featured calendar event model with:

  • Cal.com booking ID linkage
  • Email-to-event association
  • Attendees as JSONB array
  • Source tracking (manual, calcom, email_detected)
  • Detection confidence score (for AI-detected meetings)

docs.title: stream_state
docs.text: Tracks YouTube live streams with video ID, status, viewer count, peak viewers, and raw API data.

docs.title: stream_announcements
docs.text:
Records cross-platform announcement delivery with retry support:

Column Type Description platform VARCHAR(50) discord, x, instagram, facebook announcement_type VARCHAR(50) go_live, end_stream, milestone status VARCHAR(20) pending, sent, failed retry_count INTEGER Delivery attempt count

docs.title: platform_credentials
docs.text: Stores platform connection details (YouTube API key, Discord webhook, X OAuth2, Meta token).

docs.title: channel_voice_settings
docs.text:
Per-platform AI tone configuration:

Column Description tone_preset professional, friendly, hype, mixed custom_prompt User's full prompt override core_hashtags Base hashtags array cta_text Call-to-action text templates Caption template JSONB

docs.title: milestone_thresholds
docs.text: Subscriber milestone targets (100, 500, 1K, 5K, 10K, 50K) with celebration tracking.
